response.justification: Bogdan is an exceptionally strong senior engineer with 19 years of software development, solid developer tools experience (Altom CI/CD infrastructure, NI IDE work, recent Stripe MCP tooling), and clear thought leadership. However, the position requires 2+ years of people management experience and 3 years of technical leadership in a formal capacity. His resume shows zero evidence of managing people, conducting performance reviews, hiring, or team leadership—all non-negotiable for a manager role. His career is firmly on the individual contributor track advancing to Staff Engineer positions rather than people leadership. This is a fundamental mismatch for a mid-to-senior management position.
strengths: 19 years of progressive software engineering experience with Staff-level technical roles, demonstrating mastery and advancement as a senior engineer
Solid developer tools and infrastructure background including CI/CD systems (Altom), IDE development (National Instruments), and modern developer tooling (MCP at Stripe)
Proven thought leadership through public talks, workshops (Web Internals course, Selenium training), and significant open-source projects addressing developer pain points
concerns: Critical gap: zero people management experience; resume shows no evidence of managing reports, conducting 1-on-1s, performance reviews, or team leadership required for this manager role
Career trajectory strongly favors individual contributor advancement (multiple Staff Engineer positions) over management track; no mention of mentoring or developing team members
Limited formal technical leadership evidence; does not demonstrate architectural decisions or strategic influence across teams required for a manager role
